programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Too Hot to Handle
programme.desc: Donatello tells the other three Turtles that, based on his calculations, the Earth has changed its orbit and is heading for the Sun.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Nightmare in the Lair
programme.desc: Down in the sewers, while Michelangelo, Leonardo, and Raphael are watching yet another cheesy sci-fi film, Donatello is busy building the dream-o-vision.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Phantom of the Sewers
programme.desc: After another night's performance of the robotic Willie Wombat Revue at the Willie Wombat Revue Theatre, a mysterious figure, wearing a long black cape, black fedora, white face mask, and known only as the Phantom, makes an appearance.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Leonardo is Missing
programme.desc: When Donatello, Michelangelo, and Raphael return from the video arcade they discover that Donatello's lab is in disarray and Leonardo is missing.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Snakes Alive!
programme.desc: While practicing their martial arts, Leonardo tells Splinter that he is convinced that there is one combat maneuver he has yet to teach the Turtles.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Polly Wanna Pizza
programme.desc: Michelangelo returns from his sojourn to the pet shop with a monkey named Clyde. After only a few minutes with the undisciplined pet Leonardo, Donatello and Raphael demand that it be returned to the pet shop.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Mr. Nice Guy
programme.desc: Burne assigns April a story reporting on the increase in urban crime. The only problem is that urban crime is decreasing.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Sleuth on the Loose
programme.desc: While the Turtles are watching their favorite TV show April interrupts the broadcast with a special bulletin about a break-in at Dynamic Science Labs.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Rust Never Sleeps
programme.desc: Krang works on an oxidizer rocket with enough power to blast the Technodrome to Earth, but something goes wrong, and instead of thrust, the device grows rust.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): A Real Snow Job
programme.desc: The Turtles are holidaying in the Alps, as Splinter goes off to a nearby mountain to meditate. April is in an Alpine village with Irma when she notices the ice and snow melting, unusual for this time of year.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have got their hands on some white beans which have very strange properties, when mixed with soda anyone who eats them turns into a bouncing ball. Grizzy realises that if he wants to have a nap, he must get rid of the magic beans.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly wants to get rid of the old piano in the garage at any cost, for the Lemmings love using it to make a dissonant racket. But when he notices a she-bear he's in love with likes the melody of Fur Elise, he decides to learn the piece.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings inherit a crate that fell off a Canadian army convoy transporting ultra-secret material. The case inside the crate emits a sort of laser beam that divides objects into multiple miniaturised versions of themselves.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Near the supermarket, a machine hands out surprise eggs in exchange for a coin. Amongst the lots to be won, there is a super firework rocket the Lemmings dream of.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: At the Ranger's home, Grizzy finds a high-tech gadget that enables you to learn anything within seconds thanks to electrodes hooked to the brain, meaning that he can become the perfect bear.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: An old metal box found by chance in a recess of a cliff brings back childhood memories for Grizzy and the Lemmings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy struggles to watch a documentary on salmon fish, nice and comfy on the sofa, as the Lemmings play chemistry novices with a fun experiment box.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's TV remote control is stolen by a partridge who collects high-tech objects in his nest perched high up in a tree. This is a case of absolute urgency.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy has got it into his head to offer a butterfly to the bear he's in love with. But the Lemmings fight with him over the rare specimens he's spotted.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have discovered an addictive new game based on the astonishing bouncing properties of the ranger's new running shoes.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy babysits for the she-bear he has a crush on. There's no question of Grizzy tripping up, as he has no intention of taking any risks, but he hasn't reckoned on the Lemmings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Unwilling to share the food both he and the Lemmings are wild about, Grizzy locks it up inside the ranger's brand new and apparently burglar-proof safe.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is impressed by the TV show of a magician who transforms a partridge into a bouquet of flowers. To get rid of the Lemmings once and for all, for they exasperate him with their incessant games, Grizzy tries the magic trick on them.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's TV remote control is stolen by a partridge who collects high tech objects. This is a case of absolute urgency. Grizzy has no other choice but to requisition the Lemmings' propulsion game to recover his possession.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have discovered a truly wild new game, an augmented reality programme on the ranger's digital tablet. Grizzy confiscates this toy and becomes a fan of the salmon fishing game.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The only edible food item left in the ranger's cabin is a salmon stuck inside a block of ice. Starving, Grizzy decides to melt the ice with the blow dryer. Only the Lemmings are already using it for their latest rocket game in the piping system.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have unearthed a box filled with birthday fountains of light that spit forth giant bursts of sparks. They have a blast, but their new game is not really compatible with a bear's nap.

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash hears of a training ground for wild Charizard and decides it's just the place to hone his own Charizard’s strength. But the tough lessons to be learned there will change Ash and Charizard’s friendship forever!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Back on the road to the Azalea Gym, our heroes discover a Sunflora festival in a nearby town and meet a Trainer whose Sunflora is too sad to compete. Can Ash and the gang put a smile on its face?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash is having a hard time getting his Chikorita to listen to him, and Nurse Joy thinks that it’s due to a bout of jealousy over Ash’s friendship with Pikachu! Can Ash prove how much Chikorita means to him?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Our heroes meet a Trainer whose Hoppip partners can predict the weather. But on the horizon, there are two storms approaching...and one of them goes by the name 'Team Rocket'!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: The legendary superhero Gligarman grows old and is beginning to consider hanging up his cape. When Ash and friends wander into town, they bring with them a challenge that could be his final fight: Team Rocket!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: When an unruly herd of Mareep ruin their lunch plans, our heroes decide to help their timid herder in time to compete in a local battle festival.

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: When the top student at a Pokémon training center loses interest in Pokémon battles, opting to build a comprehensive Pokémon database instead, it’s up to Ash to convince him that battling is not just about probability!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: When Ash finds a Cyndaquil in the wild, he is eager to catch it...but you can bet he’s not the only one!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Pen-Y-Lan House P2
programme.desc: Stuart investigates the main house alone, although it isn’t long before he feels something is there with him.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Bishton Hall
programme.desc: Yvette takes Glen and Mary into the incredibly haunted Bishton Hall. They start in the upper floors and work their way down to the cellars, but is something following them.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Margam Castle P1
programme.desc: Karl investigates the ground floor back rooms of this landmark castle alone, but soon realises that something is lurking in the shadows.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Margam Castle P2

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Dorchester Prison 2 P1
programme.desc: Said to be the Most Haunted prison in the UK, HMP Dorchester is a scary place to be with a group of people but alone it becomes terrifying. Karl investigates the upper cells and gets more than he bargained for.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Dorchester Prison 2 P2
programme.desc: Stuart enters the underbelly of this remarkable building and starts to witness some very strange occurrences.

programme.title: Murdertown: Barnsley
programme.desc: In 2015, a 69-year-old man is found stabbed in his home. At the scene, forensics uncover a receipt from a nearby cashpoint. CCTV at the bank reveals a woman using the victim's card to withdraw cash. Who is she? And how does she relate to the murder?

programme.title: Murdertown: Pontypridd
programme.desc: In 2014, a woman goes missing after a night drinking in the local pubs. CCTV finds her walking home arm in arm with another drinker. He just happens to be a former butcher. Police soon realise he has put his trade to use by dismembering his victim.

programme.title: Murdertown: Tamworth
programme.desc: In 1994, a gang kill a woman who manages a building society and tie up her husband, before robbing the building society of large sums of cash. But despite making a tearful plea for help, the injured husband’s story doesn’t tally.

programme.title: Murdertown: Watford
programme.desc: In 2000, a young woman is stabbed to death while walking her dog in a picturesque park in the town. A suspect is seen running semi-naked across the M1 motorway. It takes detectives weeks to identify the killer as a local man called Leon Amos.

programme.title: Murdertown: Huddersfield
programme.desc: In 2014 a sex offender called Robert Hind goes missing from a bail hostel. Cops fear he’s absconded to commit crime. The town is scoured. But then the cops learn that the sex offender hasn’t absconded - he’s been murdered.

programme.title: Murdertown: Sunderland
programme.desc: Throughout the early '90s young boys would be found dead after sniffing glue and being trapped in derelict buildings which caught fire. Cops soon found they were actually being systematically murdered by a man called Steven Grieveson.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder
programme.desc: Carole and Harry Jarvis were married for over 30 years. But he met a new woman, heiress Rita Heyster, and decided it was time to move on. In a bizarre, twisted scheme, Harry and Rita plotted to murder Carole and cover up her death.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder
programme.desc: William Walker was a firefighter and paramedic with a reputation for helping strangers. Little wonder that debt-ridden Uloma Curry was attracted to him. Within weeks of their wedding she was plotting to get her hands on his cash.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder
programme.desc: Micah Rine's family had a bad feeling about Thomas Pate. He seemed inauthentic. He had a troubled past. But nothing could persuade Micah that he wasn't the man of her dreams. By the time Micah realised Thomas had a dark side, it was too late.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder
programme.desc: Rod Covlin met Shele Danishevsky at a Jewish singles night. She was older, successful and rich, and Rod was happy to live off his wife. After her mysterious death, it seemed he would stop at nothing to get his hands on her money.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder
programme.desc: From the moment she set eyes on co-worker Charlie Stephens, Kirstin was determined to have him; never mind his 40-year marriage. She seduced him, and in doing so set her husband on the path of financial ruin, deception, and ultimately murder.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder
programme.desc: When Faye and Marian Caliman first met, she was bowled over by his gentlemanly ways. He was the life and soul of family parties. But when the marriage turned sour, Faye and her family realised Marian was not the true gent he’d always pretended.

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: Deadly Envy
programme.desc: An aspiring Hollywood actress is reacquainted with an old friend and fellow actress who becomes deeply jealous of her beauty and success.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Lust
programme.desc: One morning in the stairwell of a Toronto building, a guard makes a grotesque discovery: the dead body of a Filipina nanny. It's clear she's been savagely murdered and raped.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Model Killer
programme.desc: A gorgeous young model is bludgeoned to death in her downtown apartment. The wounds to her head are so severe police initially presume she's been shot.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Dead End
programme.desc: A young couple hits the road, hitchhiking across North America. Free spirits keen to explore the world. When the young woman stops phoning home, her father is alarmed.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Bull's Eye
programme.desc: A Girl Guide leader is found dead on the floor of her bedroom. When an autopsy reveals a bullet lodged in her brain, detectives learn they’re investigating a murder.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Burning Obsession
programme.desc: In 1988, a promiscuous 22-year old boy from the Sikh community has one affair too many and ends up losing his life. Diligent detective work and DNA help police nail the killer who believed he had gotten away with murder.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Who Killed Santa?
programme.desc: In 2001, there are a series of murders of homeless people in Toronto. The latest killing is of a gentle man, well known in the area by residents who nicknamed him Santa Claus.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Angel of Death
programme.desc: An angel of mercy turns out to be an angel of death when the number of deaths in an Indiana Intensive Care Unit skyrockets. One astute nurse believes there must be more to this than just natural causes.

programme.title: 72 Hours: The Ghost
programme.desc: A cunning arsonist stays one step ahead of the police and terrifies a small farming community until behavioural profiling help detectives zero in on a very unlikely suspect.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Loch Ness Monster
programme.desc: In 1998, in a normally peaceful suburb, a pervert on a bicycle appears, performing sexual acts on himself and then attacking teenage girls. For reasons apparent to the residents, they nickname him after the Scottish Loch Ness monster.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole stop in Delaware, where they help their new host family with a space problem, while their internship is at a lively daycare center.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole plan competing weddings for a lesbian couple.

programme.title: The Simple Life
programme.desc: Paris and Nicole continue their travels through Maryland, living with a host family who Paris offers to cook for, and working at a bakery.

programme.title: The Simple Life

programme.title: The Simple Life

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan's efforts to help a German baroness to escape are thwarted by Klink, who has arranged for the transfer of a British prisoner who is senior to Colonel Hogan.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes helps to track down a killer who leaves clues next to the women he kills.

programme.desc: The detectives create a plan to trap a killer before he murders his 7th wife.

programme.desc: PRS and Psychic/medium Michelle Belanger investigate a Pennsylvania home; where a mother named Kathy and her son Michael have both felt the presence of a young boy and hear voices calling, "Mom?" but no one is there.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Ton's Got a Gun

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Fall Guys

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Haff-Inated

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Battle Bought

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Jersey Score

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Miami Heat

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Curveball

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Intensive care

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: No looking back
programme.desc: Leah gets the results of Ali's MRI and is relieved to find out there's nothing wrong with her brain. Meanwhile, Jenelle is spending time with Jace and finally at peace with her mum.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Kailyn adjusts to being alone for the holidays, Jenelle regrets filing charges against Kieffer and Leah works to make the holidays special for the twins.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Lean on me
programme.desc: Leah and Corey take Ali to the geneticist and get some troubling news. Jenelle and Kieffer face court; Chelsea learns a friend is pregnant, and Jo is not around for Issac.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Breaking Point
programme.desc: Kieffer gets upset that Jenelle hung out with an old boyfriend while he was in jail and Leah gets the results of Ali's genetic testing. Also Kailyn gets into a car accident.

programme.title: The Maury Show: Is My Sister's Boyfriend My Baby's Dad?
programme.desc: Denese and her mom return because they know Dejon fathered Denese’s daughter! Plus, Rebecca has been here 6 times for 7 DNA tests and returns for her 8th and 9th tests!

programme.title: The Maury Show: 7 Babies. 8 Men. Who's the Father?
programme.desc: These moms are desperate to find their children's missing fathers. But these denying dads say that there's no way they're the real fathers. When the DNA results are revealed, who will be proven right and who will be still searching for answers?

programme.title: The Maury Show: He's My Husband Now. Not Your Baby's Dad!
programme.desc: These guests say they're positive they know the true identity of the father of their children, but they just need a DNA test to prove it. Now the results are about to be revealed, but will they be the results they were hoping for?

programme.title: The Maury Show: I Cheated with 97 Women. Will My Fiancé Forgive Me?
programme.desc: Did Rey cheat on Ashley while she was pregnant with his baby and have a secret threesome behind her back? Plus, Jason is worried that his uncle is paying his fiancé to sleep with him. His uncle has a shocking confession that you have to see!

programme.title: The Maury Show: The Maury Show
programme.desc: It’s a Spring Wild Safari Spectacular on today’s MAURY! Jungle Jack Hanna joins Maury to share some of the most amazing animals we’ve ever seen! Your whole family will want to tune in to watch this amazing episode of MAURY!

programme.title: The Maury Show: We Had A Threesome…But I'm Not Your Baby's Dad!
programme.desc: These couples decided to have a threesome which ended up in a pregnancy. Now that the babies are born, these men deny they are the fathers. Today they will be forced to face the truth.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 3
programme.desc: The kids return to Omashu so that Aang can learn Earthbending from King Bumi; however, when they get there, they are shocked to find the city is now in Fire Nation hands.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 4
programme.desc: When the kids end up in a mysterious and strange swamp, their fears are exposed. And Aang learns about being `connected to the Earth' from an unlikely teacher.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 5
programme.desc: Aang and the gang discover an Earth town that celebrates `Avatar Day'. They learn that it is not a celebration of the Avatar, but a festival of anger for something Aang did in his past.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 6
programme.desc: In search for an Earthbending teacher for Aang, the kids go to an underground Earthbending tournament where they encounter Toph, a blind, but powerful Earthbender.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 7

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 8
programme.desc: As the kids are pursued relentlessly by a mysterious machine, their exhaustion puts them at each other's throats.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 9

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 10

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 11

programme.title: UFOTV Presents: The Mysterious Stone Monuments of Markawasi Peru
programme.desc: Travel high into the Peruvian Andes to explore the strange, wondrous and unexplained mysteries of stone formations that may have been carved by thousands of years of erosion -- or the hands of a long-dead civilization

programme.title: UFOTV Presents: The Afterlife Investigations
programme.desc: Narrated by Britain's leading investigative Journalist - Donal MacIntyre - this award winning film presents four never before seen scientific investigations into life after death.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS AND UNSOLVED DISSAPEARANCES
programme.desc: Legally declared dead, bodies never found. Disappearances spark speculation, media attention, and unanswered questions. Where did they go?

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET TECHNOLOGIES
programme.desc: Scientists, engineers, military, and intelligence agencies push boundaries with unlimited budgets and secrecy. This episode unveils known (or suspected) top-secret projects.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: THE MYSTERY AND CULTS OF MUMMIES
programme.desc: Unlock the mystery of mummification from ancient Egypt to present. Explore its fascination and quest for Queen Nefertiti's mummy. Where did 70 million ancient mummies disappear? Delve into Latin American mummy legends.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: UNSOLVED MYSTERIES IN PHYSICS
programme.desc: In the late 19th century, scientists thought all major physics laws were discovered. Now, we realize vast unknowns remain. Despite our understanding, mysteries persist in the physical world.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On October 11, 1998, Dr. Harold Shipman is tried for murdering fifteen elderly patients. Exhumation and evidence of lethal morphine lead to his conviction. He is later found to have killed 215 people.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, Ashley Pond and Miranda Gaddis disappear in Portland. Neighbor Ward Weaver, a suspect with a violent history, is later found guilty of their murders and sentenced to life in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: A picture-perfect Canadian couple Karla Homolka and Paul Bernardo join to perform torture and mutilation on young victims - crimes that are virtually unspeakable. Two teenage girls were raped and murdered, and the couple even filmed themselves doing it.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In July 2000, Sarah Payne is abducted and murdered. Roy Whiting, a convicted sex offender, is found guilty and sentenced to life in 2001. Sarah’s case leads to the implementation of “Sarah’s Law” in the UK in 2011.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 27, 2005, Dennis Rader, known as the BTK killer, pleads guilty to ten murders he committed in Wichita, Kansas, from 1974 to 1994. After a journalist's article reignites the case, Rader is arrested and sentenced to ten life terms without parole.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In September 1987, Simon Dale is murdered. His ex-wife, Susan Wilberforce, is acquitted of the crime but later convicted of fraud and sentenced to seven years. The murder remains unsolved.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Nathaniel Bar-Jonah, a paedophile, is convicted in 2002 for multiple crimes but not for the 1996 disappearance of Zachary Ramsay. Zachary’s body is never found, and Bar-Jonah dies in prison in 2008.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, fashion journalist Christa Worthington is found murdered in her Truro home. Christopher McCowen is convicted in 2006 of her murder, robbery, and rape, despite his claims of innocence and other DNA found at the scene.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2001, British traveler Peter Falconio is presumed murdered in the Australian outback. Joanne Lees survives the attack, and Bradley John Murdoch is convicted of the murder in 2005. Peter's body is never found.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Boiling Lake
programme.desc: Tucked away, high in the mountains of the Caribbean island of Dominica lies a marvel of nature  a lake whose water literally boils and steams like a witch's cauldron.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Wild Water
programme.desc: In this episode, George investigates the wild side of water in dangerous class 5 river rapids, a flooded cave, and even in the ocean surrounded by massive Great White Sharks.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Midwest Stormfest
programme.desc: This season the tornadoes begin early - in February. Through the season, George follows the big weather from Missouri west to the Dakotas, and dodges the huge, destructive Greensburg tornado in southern Kansas.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Island Caving
programme.desc: Accompanied by a group of hardcore cavers from Alberta and British Columbia, George descends into spectacular caves that lead deep into the earth on Vancouver Island. The team carries inflatable kayaks to explore a dark underground river.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hurricane
programme.desc: Linking up with legendary storm chaser Jim Leonard, George flies to Jamaica to track Hurricane Dean. George then travels to Nova Scotia to experience the even bigger turmoil of Hurricane Noel at the iconic Maritime village of Peggy's Cove.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Waterspouts
programme.desc: In the Florida Keys, George uses a motorboat, a van and small plane to get up close to waterspouts - the nautical cousins of tornadoes. He also meets some of the 25 million alligators that live in the Sunshine State.

programme.title: Naked Science: Anatomy Of A Hurricane
programme.desc: Hurricanes are one of the most destructive weather events to strike our planet and have the same power as a nuclear weapon.

programme.title: Naked Science: Death Of The Earth
programme.desc: The earth is 4.5 billion years old and it is slowly dying. Tectonic activity is slowly cooling the planet – the continents will eventually move together to form a new supercontinent called Pangea Ultima.

programme.title: Moon Shots: Inside the Lost Apollo Archive
programme.desc: The world famous Apollo missions are some of the greatest feats ever accomplished by man. With rare archive footage and unprecedented access to life on board the Apollo spacecrafts, this stunning one-hour special brings scientific history to life.

programme.title: Neil Armstrong: One Small Step
programme.desc: Neil Armstrong has stamped his place in history by becoming the first person to walk on the moon.

programme.title: The Apollo Experience: Apollo 17: Part 1
programme.desc: Between 1960 and 1972, mankind boldly went where no human had been before. Across 11 manned missions, 24 men flew to the moon and 12 walked upon its surface. This film documents Apollo 17 through the eyes of the astronauts who flew.

programme.title: The Apollo Experience: Apollo 17: Part 2
programme.desc: In this episode, hear first hand testimonials and dialogue from the incredible men who walked on the moon. Incredible footage and photos give a unique and beautiful experience of their data collection process and journey home.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Join Brian Greene on a wild ride into the weird realm of quantum physics, which governs the universe on the tiniest of scales.

programme.title: Wildest Islands: Amazon River Islands: The Floating Forest
programme.desc: The Anavilhanas and Mariua archipelagos are deep in the heart of the Amazon rainforest. Flooded for 6 months of the year, the islands are a fortress of submerged trees. / Vancouver island is the largest island off the west coast of North America.

programme.title: Wildest Islands
programme.desc: Islands can be home to the most extreme examples of life, the wildest of people and the most dramatic landscapes. Natural selection fuels evolution in the most dramatic way

programme.title: Australia's Wild Places: The Island of the Devil
programme.desc: Just 460,000 people live in Tasmania, an island off the southeast coast of mainland Australia. While its human population is small, the majestic state is rich in natural wonders and is known for its large tracts of pristine wilderness.

programme.title: Wildest Latin America: Pantanal: Brazil's Wild Heart

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Roy Choi’s food reflects a new American idea of "natural" fusion. Bourdain examines the meeting point of Asian, Mexican, Latino culture in modern LA.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown: Punjab, India

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Tony and his friend, world-renowned chef Eric Ripert, explore the far reaches of indigenous Andes in search of a rare variety of wild cocoa that is said to be the "best" in the world.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown: Mississippi Delta
programme.desc: Tony seeks to challenge his notions of Mississippi by taking a road trip to meet a cast of Mississippians enjoying the diverse foodways and embracing the traditional and contemporary music worlds.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Detroit holds a unique place in American history. Few cities have seen such a dramatic rise and fall from economic boom to disparity. Tony explores the past, present and future of the Motor City.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Mother Knows Best
programme.desc: Dog has two fugitives to catch, and he gets help from the unlikeliest sources - the fugitives' mothers. First, Ginger's mother wants to revoke her daughter's bond. Then, Brandon's mother decides to revoke his bond because Brandon has an ice habit.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Irons in the Fire
programme.desc: Dog is back hunting in the big city of Denver, but chasing fugitives on the mainland is more dangerous than ever. So Dog decides that the crew should get pepper-ball guns, which have much better range than pepper spray.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Rocky Mountain Roundup
programme.desc: Dog decides to help two of his bondsmen buddies, Dave "Red" Widhelm and "Downtown" Bobby Brown. First up is Red, who has a client, named Alvin, whose bond is $20,000. Then the team goes after Craig, a client of Bobby's whose bond is almost $70,000.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: This One's For You
programme.desc: Dog leads the team on another adrenaline-filled chase in Denver. The fugitive, Robert, has a habit of address-hopping that makes him a difficult target, but Dog refuses to give up and finally tracks Robert down in his latest hideaway.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Baby Lyssa Steps Up
programme.desc: The team is still in Colorado, and today Dog starts his day by to giving his kids and crew a sample of their western heritage by taking them horseback riding. This hunt takes Dog back to his old stomping grounds in Denver.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Running on Empty
programme.desc: Dog's time is running out in Colorado. After a month of helping his bail bondsmen friends around Denver, he's got to get his family back home to Hawaii. But Dog wants one more capture before they leave.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Inside Out
programme.desc: Dog kickstarts the search for Jackson, a 240lbs, six feet tall bad boy who has skipped out on a $10,000 bond. Jackson’s apparently at his father-in-law's house, but the father-in-law has info about Jackson that brings Dog to a unique hideout.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Growing Up Is Hard To Do

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Year of the Dog

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: The Mystery of Mona Lisa

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Brand of Brothers

programme.desc: A confrontation erupts in a Virginia alleyway when a driver yells at a woman for allegedly blocking her car; in Atlanta, a man on a bike leads multiple police cruisers on a high-speed chase; a hydroplaning accident in Louisiana.

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: In Wooster, Ohio, a road rage incident escalates when one of the drivers follows the other back to his home; a driver in Georgia hits a stopped tow truck's ramp and soars over 120 feet; a fender bender in a Texas parking lot spins out of control.

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: On a rural highway in Iowa, a driver and his passenger both fall asleep while on the road; in a San Francisco parking lot, a woman berates another driver who she claims parked too close to her car.

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: At a red light in Arizona, a car sideswipes the door on another driver's vehicle during a heated road rage encounter; two drivers get into a yelling match as they attempt to squeeze by each other on a narrow street in the Boston suburbs.

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: In Florida, a bat-wielding motorist goes after another driver; a senior citizen in North Carolina threatens to lie to police after allegedly backing into another car; an ATV rider in St. Louis loses control.

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries with Dennis Farina
programme.desc: This episode includes: Burned Evidence, Dark Dante, Skeleton Canyon, Con/Doc and Smitty.

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries with Dennis Farina

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries with Dennis Farina
programme.desc: This episode includes: Voodoo Victims, Trailer Terror, Practical Joker, Pizza Man, Gulf POW Dream and Accountant Amnesia.

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries with Dennis Farina

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries with Dennis Farina

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries with Dennis Farina

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: The dinos each receive separate phone calls inviting them to receive a prestigious award: Dino Of The Year! This sets off a series of arguments, with each dino declaring that they must be the winner.

programme.title: Storm Hawks
programme.desc: Everyone’s worried when Junko agrees to look after a baby Wallop for the day.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: Jet wakes up one day to find an oversized egg in his bed and is convinced he laid it while he was sleeping. The Dinos try to convince him that this is not only impossible, but just plain weird.

programme.title: Storm Hawks
programme.desc: A double-crossing explorer has found a way inside the ancient Forbidden City, which means Snipe will get his hands on the treasure, unless the Storm Hawks can get there first.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: When Skor, Skrap and the Links infiltrate the museum Trojan Horse-style through a newly purchased giant ancient statue, the dinos find themselves at the mercy of Skor’s semi-automatic plasma canon.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Korg's theft of a scroll from Naroom's library sends our heroes in pursuit of the Shadow Magi to the forbidding desert realm of the Sands of D’Resh. They ride aboard a Quanjuel – a horse-like creature that can travel through magical vortexes.

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Master Mayhem receives a visit from… himself! From the future?! With two MM’s in the mix, Earth is destined for doom... if they could just stop arguing. Meanwhile RoBro flexes his mechanical muscles in a Monster Mashdown that’ll go down in history.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: It’s the inaugural run of a revolutionary new form of ground travel - a fusion-powered train that can move at extraordinary speeds.

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: Dr. Piranoid creates a new Seaviate hench-creature - Killamari, a gene-slammed Giant Squid with a venomous sting.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Dueling for Answers
programme.desc: Playmaker battles Zaizen to unlock Sol Tech’s databank and the secrets to his past!

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: When Skor discovers a crystal hidden in the Extreme Fighting Championship Belt, he devises a plan to get it. Since only humans are allowed to compete, he tricks Edgar into becoming his protégé.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse investigates strange goings-on at Stonehenge only to battle his devilish alter-ego.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Bill and Ted return to Medieval times in England so they can bring the Princess babes back to sing with the Wyld Stallions at the school dance.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Pink Or Consequences
programme.desc: Pink Panther becomes an unwitting contestant on a game show to win a year's supply of his favorite beverage.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Pink Pink Pink Pink
programme.desc: Wackiness ensues when Pink Panther's music machine creates three offbeat multiples of him.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback has placed a large number of sand pyramids all over England, threatening to sink the island Danger Mouse travels to the Sahara Desert to stop him.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: '14 Karat Pink': The Panther tries to claim the Lollapalooza Gold Vein but Manly Man has other ideas. 'Robo-Pink': While working as a Security Guard at a super secret factory, the Panther thwarts the Dogfather's plans to steal a secret 'doodad.'

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Dr Crumhorn returns and uses his transformation pills to turn himself into a little girl in an attempt to persuade Danger Mouse and Penfold to help her out and eventually lead them to break into Fort Knox.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The Earth is flooded with instant custard. Danger Mouse and Penfold travel to outer space to find the Custard Mite of Glut, a small creature that can eat up the custard, but the planet that houses the Mite is controlled by armoured aliens.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: Pink, Pink and Away': Super Pink flies into action when the Dogfather robs a bank and then accidentally kidnaps a little boy. 'Down on the Ant Farm': The Panther's experimental ant farm, complete with Ant, is accidentally delivered to the Aardvark.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Rock Me Pink': While trying to get a heavy metal rock star to play his song, Pinky becomes the concert's main attraction. 'Pinkus Pantherus': Pink joins the Roman Legion Army and ends up with the vindictive Manly Man as his commanding officer.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: In 'Big Top Pinky,' Pinky dreams of stardom in the circus and discovers his calling is as a clown. And in 'Yeti 'nother Bigfoot Story,' custodian Pinky, after running a museum display, travels to Alaska in search of Bigfoot.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 11
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a three bedroom Victorian mid-terrace house in Cheshire, a Wiltshire village-based plot offering planning permission and finally a Victorian two-up, two-down in Leeds.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander visit homes in Yorkshire, Essex and Norwich.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy view a two-bedroom in Lancashire, a shop in Middlesex and a little flat in Newcastle.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 2
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a hidden gem in Derby, a property in West London and a house in Hastings.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Tv-Free Week/Night Fright
programme.desc: The kids and parents of Ellwood City have all pledged not to watch TV for one whole week. Can they last? Then, big, brave Binky Barnes would be toast if people ever discovered he can't sleep without a night light.

programme.title: Arthur: Binky Barnes, Art Expert/Arthur's Lucky Pencil
programme.desc: It's a nightmare--Arthur and Buster have to work with Binky. On an art project! I mean, what does a bully like Binky know about art? Then, Arthur discovers the secret to success--a pencil that brings good luck to whoever possesses it.

programme.title: Arthur: Big Horns George/Bleep
programme.desc: George is inspired to write and perform his own blues song when he's introduced to the music of Koko Taylor. And, D.W. overhears a mysterious word she doesn’t know the meaning of. Can D.W. risk the consequences of blurting out <"BLEEP!">?

programme.title: Arthur: Muffy's Art Attack/Tales from the Crib
programme.desc: When Muffy sees a kinetic sculptor’s machines, she brags that she can make even better ones. Then, Vicita is upset at having to trade in her cherished crib for a big-girl bed, so D.W. shares her story of that same rite of passage.

programme.title: Arthur: The Wheel Deal/The Buster Report
programme.desc: After an injury, Brain has to spend some time using a wheelchair. Will he be able to compete in a basketball tournament for charity? The kids are assigned to write a report on someone they admire, and George picks….Buster!

programme.title: Arthur: Unfinished/D.W., Bossy Boots
programme.desc: Arthur is completely riveted by a book and can't wait to see how it ends. But the last few pages are missing! Now what? For D.W., there's nothing better than getting her own way--that is, until all her friends stop playing with her.

programme.title: Arthur: The Director's Cut/Crime and Consequences
programme.desc: After meeting his hero, Hollywood director Tufton Sinclair, George is inspired to make a movie of his own. When George accidentally breaks the class's prize woodworking project, Binky gets unfairly blamed.

programme.title: Arthur: Carried Away/Dueling Detectives!
programme.desc: Kate and Mei Lin are thrilled to tour the solar system with Pal's cousin, Dr. Yowl. Wally is missing and Elwood City's best detective is on the case. And that detective is…Binky?!?

programme.title: Arthur: Muffy's Soccer Shocker/Brother, Can You Spare A Clarinet?
programme.desc: Muffy's proud that her dad is the new soccer coach--until he exhausts the team with demanding drills. The try-outs for the Young Person's Orchestra are coming to Lakewood Elementary and everyone agrees that Binky is a shoe-in.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: It's Your Birthday, Barney
programme.desc: Every day with Barney is fun—but today is extra special. It's Barney's birthday!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Layla and her friends celebrate her birthday in the park with foods that aren’t traditionally American.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: On Again, Off Again
programme.desc: Barney, Baby Bop and the kids learn all about "on" and "off" by playing games together.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ, Baby Bop and Riff can hardly wait for the petting zoo that is coming to the park. Riff feels terrible when he accidentally wrecks BJ’s beloved scooter.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Tea-riffic Manners
programme.desc: A tea party planned by the children turns into a fun opportunity to reinforce lessons about good manners! Throughout the day, Baby Bop is given helpful advice.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: All Aboard
programme.desc: Kami, one of Barney's youngest friends, has trouble keeping up with the other kids and she's tired of always being last. She's not having a very good day.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Pistachio - BJ Snack Attack
programme.desc: Barney is snacking on pistachios...and just can't seem to stop! His young friends try to help by hiding them from Barney. When that doesn't work, they tell him a story to help him stop eating pistachios.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: A New Friend
programme.desc: While Barney has fun with his friends every day, there's something special about making a brand NEW friend! When Mr. Boyd's niece, Colleen visits the park, she's eager to make friends with the other kids...but being the "new kid" isn't easy.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: The Emperor's Contest - The Whole Truth
programme.desc: Everyone is excited about a fishing contest that is being held in the park; everyone except Ryan, who doesn't want to follow the contest rules. When Ryan is tempted not to follow the rules, Barney tells a story called "The Emperor's Contest."

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): A Real Snow Job
programme.desc: The Turtles are holidaying in the Alps, as Splinter goes off to a nearby mountain to meditate. April is in an Alpine village with Irma when she notices the ice and snow melting, unusual for this time of year.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Venice on the Half-Shell
programme.desc: Krang decides to use a hydrofluxer device to flood Venice, holding it for ransom.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Artless
programme.desc: Two snobbish art-collecting aliens arrive in Florence and begin stealing great works of art.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Ring of Fire
programme.desc: Krang sends Shredder to set up a special lens that will focus the sun's rays to create a devastating heat ray.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Irish Jig Is Up
programme.desc: Krang and Shredder decide to use Ireland as their base for the Technodrome. With a device called the Rainbow Trans-Charmer, the villains intend to turn harmless little creatures like sheep and rabbits into slavering beasts.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Shredder's New Sword
programme.desc: In Dimension X Krang is using a long-range scanner and discovers a rare metal buried in a ruined castle in England. The metal turns out to be King Arthur's legendary sword, Excalibur.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Lost Queen of Atlantis
programme.desc: Shredder, Rocksteady and Bebop are searching for a valuable energy source in Athens and discover that it is April's new necklace.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles on the Orient Express
programme.desc: April is covering the revival of the Orient Express. Shredder is on the train with Rocksteady and Bebop carrying the supercharger, a device to accelerate the train to super speed.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): April Gets in Dutch
programme.desc: April is covering the showing of the world-famed Duchess Diamond. Krang wants the diamond, a flawless stone, to focus the rays on his laser dimension blade.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Northern Lights Out
programme.desc: Eric Red seizes both April and Professor Swenson's map to the underground volcanoes in Scandinavia to tap into the molten energy and melt the polar ice caps.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Elementary, My Dear Turtle

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy babysits for the she-bear he has a crush on. There's no question of Grizzy tripping up, as he has no intention of taking any risks, but he hasn't reckoned on the Lemmings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Unwilling to share the food both he and the Lemmings are wild about, Grizzy locks it up inside the ranger's brand new and apparently burglar-proof safe.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's TV remote control is stolen by a partridge who collects high tech objects. This is a case of absolute urgency. Grizzy has no other choice but to requisition the Lemmings' propulsion game to recover his possession.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have unearthed a box filled with birthday fountains of light that spit forth giant bursts of sparks. They have a blast, but their new game is not really compatible with a bear's nap.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's last jar of chocolate spread has been pinched by the Lemmings, who are savouring it in secret.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The vibrations created by Grizzy's snoring during his nap give the Lemmings an idea for a bouncing game. But when Grizzy changes positions and his snoring stops, the Lemmings must find other sources of vibrations.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Hoarders

programme.title: Hoarders

programme.title: Hoarders

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 1

programme.desc: Glyn Dix shot dead his landlady in 1979. While serving a life sentence he met and fell in love with a fellow inmate’s mother. They married, but three years after his release he killed his wife.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan talks Klink into building an officers' club, whereby a boat can be built to sail away with an escaped British POW and the German gunsight he snagged while escaping.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan and his men must destroy a German anti-aircraft artillery unit so that the Allies can send in a bombing raid on Hitler's birthday.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: The Germans have stolen a gold shipment from the French and Hogan must convince Klink to have it moved to the safety of Stalag 13.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: UNSOLVED MYSTERIES IN PHYSICS
programme.desc: In the late 19th century, scientists thought all major physics laws were discovered. Now, we realize vast unknowns remain. Despite our understanding, mysteries persist in the physical world.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MEDICAL MYSTERIES
programme.desc: Medical history is rife with jaw-dropping oddities, miraculous recoveries, and unsolved mysteries. Beyond shock value, these cases showcase human resilience and the vast unknowns in understanding the body and mind.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET PROJECTS AND OPERATIONS
programme.desc: Imagine a world with open use of resources from secret government projects. Little is known about these black ops. They hold mysteries and consequences, challenging democratic ideals. History reveals human experimentation, contrary to official claims.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIES OF HIDDEN ROOMS
programme.desc: Throughout history, humanity has been captivated by hidden places—rooms, passages, and chambers. Whether for escape or sinister motives, these ten notable hidden spaces span the ages, revealing intrigue and mystery.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SUPRESSED INVENTIONS IN HISTORY
programme.desc: Explore lost or suppressed inventions from history, some skeptical, others mysterious. Despite doubts, many are useful today. Stay open-minded as we delve into these intriguing relics.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: HUMAN SACRIFICES
programme.desc: Human sacrifice, practiced since the Paleolithic era, was seen as a gift to gods for cosmic order. Found in black magic, voodoo, and Satanism, these rituals may hint at a mysterious connection to higher forces.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: EXTRA TERRESTRIAL
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries explores extraterrestrial phenomena like ship and aircraft vanishings, Area 51, close encounters, classified projects.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS CREATURES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries examines mysterious creatures that may live in Earth including the chupacabra, the Loch Ness Monster, the thunderbird,) the yeti or Bigfoot.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2001, British traveler Peter Falconio is presumed murdered in the Australian outback. Joanne Lees survives the attack, and Bradley John Murdoch is convicted of the murder in 2005. Peter's body is never found.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1999, NFL player Rae Carruth is convicted for orchestrating the shooting of his pregnant girlfriend. He is sentenced to 18-24 years. Their son, Chancellor, has cerebral palsy.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In February 1977, Australian heiress Janie Shepherd vanishes in London. David Lashley, a convicted rapist, later confesses to her murder in prison. In 1990, he is convicted based on this confession and sentenced to life.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1998, Peter Bergna's car crashes off a mountain, killing his wife Rinette. After suspicions arise and a first trial ends in a hung jury, Bergna is convicted of murder in 2002 following new testimony about his abusive behavior.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1975, 15-year-old Martha Moxley is murdered in Belle Haven, Connecticut. The case goes cold until 2002, when Michael Skakel, a family member, is convicted based on new evidence. Skakel maintains his innocence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2007, Meredith Kercher is murdered in Italy. Amanda Knox and Raffaele Sollecito are initially convicted but later exonerated. Rudy Guede is convicted. The Kercher family still seeks justice.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On December 7, 1993, Colin Ferguson killed six and injured nineteen in the Long Island Railroad Massacre. Defending himself at trial, he bizarrely questioned his own victims and was sentenced to over 300 years in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1995 Paris, Guy Georges, a serial killer, was linked to multiple murders through DNA and later confessed in court. He was sentenced to life in prison, leading to the establishment of a DNA database for offenders in France.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2006, Nina Sharanova vanished in Oakland. Her ex-husband, Hans Reiser, was convicted of her murder after their son’s testimony and leading police to her body. Reiser is now appealing the conviction.

programme.title: Naked Science: Death Of The Earth
programme.desc: The earth is 4.5 billion years old and it is slowly dying. Tectonic activity is slowly cooling the planet – the continents will eventually move together to form a new supercontinent called Pangea Ultima.

programme.title: Naked Science: Venus
programme.desc: Earth has an evil twin. Venus. It is shrouded in a lethal atmosphere of carbon dioxide and acid rain. The surface temperature is hot enough to melt lead and the pressure high enough to crush a nuclear submarine.

programme.title: Naked Science: How To Destroy A Planet
programme.desc: The Earth is built to last. It is a 4,550,000,000-year-old, 5,973,600,000,000,000,000,000-tonne ball of rock and iron. It has taken more devastating asteroid hits in its lifetime than you've had hot dinners, and it still orbits happily around the sun.

programme.title: Naked Science: Jupiter
programme.desc: By far the largest planet, 1300 times the size of Earth, it’s a world so enormous it could swallow every planet and moon in the solar system and still have room to spare.

programme.title: Naked Science: Diamonds
programme.desc: Naked Science follows the story of a diamond from the creation of carbon inside the heart of a dying sun, through to the cutting of a diamond mine in South Africa.

programme.title: Naked Science: Inside The Mind Of Stephen Hawking
programme.desc: Stephen Hawking, though suffering from amyotrophic lateral sclerosis has developed a unique understanding of the universe.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Join Brian Greene on a wild ride into the weird realm of quantum physics, which governs the universe on the tiniest of scales.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: What Is Space?
programme.desc: Brian Greene reveals space as a dynamic fabric that can stretch, twist, warp, and ripple under the influence of gravity, and explores dark energy, which makes up 70% of the universe.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: Universe or Multiverse?
programme.desc: Cutting-edge theories are suggesting that our universe may not be the only universe. Instead, it may be just one of an infinite number of worlds that make up the multiverse.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: The Illusion of Time
programme.desc: Brian Greene takes us on the ultimate time traveling adventure, hurtling 50 years into the future before stepping into a wormhole to travel back to Big Bang.

programme.title: EARTH´S EVIL TWIN
programme.desc: Venus is a planet closest to Earth in distance and size, it once may have been as hospitable to life as Earth is now. Then something changed and Venus became shrouded in hot dense toxic clouds.

programme.title: Meerkat Manor: Rise of the Dynasty
programme.desc: An explosion of sourgrass causes problems for everyone while Finnick makes a decision which jeopardises the pups.

programme.title: Jungle Planet
programme.desc: One of the strangest ecosystems on the planet is the Spiny Forest of Madagascar where almost all the plants are armed with thorns like daggers.

programme.title: Wild Baltic Sea: Between Finland and Sweden
programme.desc: Journey to the most unknown region of the Baltic Sea.

programme.title: Wildest Middle East
programme.desc: Wildest Middle East reveals the remotest and most surprising corners of this ancient land, where you will find nothing is as it seems and where the natural world is fighting a constant battle to survive in an ever changing world.

programme.title: Meerkat Manor: Rise of the Dynasty
programme.desc: Sencha, Munchkin and K’jess are in Flint’s line of fire and Whiskers sisters Laika and Fly babysit for the first time.

programme.title: Jungle Planet
programme.desc: The laurel forests of the Canary Islands are relics more than 20 million years old.

programme.title: Wild Asia: Two Worlds
programme.desc: A dramatic and poignant portrayal of the fragile alliances and desperate strategies for survival adopted by three species of animals caught in the no-man’s-land where the human and natural worlds collide.

programme.desc: How the Crossrail will propel the capital into the future.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Detroit holds a unique place in American history. Few cities have seen such a dramatic rise and fall from economic boom to disparity. Tony explores the past, present and future of the Motor City.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Few countries have a more contentious relationship with the United States than Iran. In this episode we heads to this Shia Muslim nation to explore a country few Americans have visited since the 80s.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Welcome to Putin’s Russia. If you don’t fall into line, you could be the target of a show trial and be thrown into a penal colony. But if you’re hungry you might actually find something worth eating.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Tony visits Andalucia during Semana Santa – Holy Week – a time filled with great pageantry and excitement. Tony’s longtime DP, Zach Zamboni, currently lives in Granada with his fiancée, Fuen Sanchez.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: In this episode of Parts Unknown Tony explores a few of the many faces of Miami. Chef Michelle Bernstein takes Tony out to an authentic Cuban breakfast and a late-night dinner at a gas station café in Doral.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: A country of only six million people where a vast percentage of the land is steaming hot jungle or a huge scrub desert known simply as The Chaco. This is one of the least known nations in the world.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: New Mexico has the largest Hispanic population per capita in the US. No better way to see the mash-up of cultures that make up this uniquely American State, than by what the people here are eating

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: The Mystery of Mona Lisa
programme.desc: When Lyssa points out that the fugitive in the next case has the same birth date as Barbara, Dog's recently deceased daughter, the team takes a special vow to capture the young woman.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Rusty Cuffs
programme.desc: Dog finds himself fighting two enemies; the law and his inner demons. He continues his legal battle to fight extradition to Mexico in connection with his 2003 capture of fugitive rapist Andrew Luster. His other struggle is private.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Make a Wish
programme.desc: Dog shows his love of justice and children in this poignant episode. He begins by giving encouragement to a group of handicapped children, then starting the search for Jacob, a fugitive who's abandoned his wife and children.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Tough Love
programme.desc: Michael is out on bail for drug sales, theft, and violation of parole. Dog's after him for failing to appear in court and his only lead is Jen, his co-signer and girlfriend.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Surprise Ending
programme.desc: Dog and the boys are going after a fugitive with a $25,000 bond and a string of theft charges behind him. When the team hits his apartment, they find a boot print on the door, a souvenir of a police raid from only days earlier?

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: The Big Wipe-Out
programme.desc: A Hawaiian surfing hero is on a wave to nowhere and it's Dog's turn to play lifeguard. "Buttons," known throughout the islands for his prowess on the waves and his daring 360's, has wiped-out on the rocky shoals of drug addiction.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: You Can Bet On It!
programme.desc: Kristine had a string of gambling and forgery charges before she jumped bail leaving Dog "holding the bag". Now it's time for Dog to call in some chits of his own, getting the help of trusty informants he's helped out over the years.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Fly Boy
programme.desc: Dog told him not to go, but Charles went anyway, skipping to Oregon against the terms of his bail. Now Dog has a tip that Charles plans to slip back into Honolulu. With $30,000 on the line Dog knows he can leave little to chance.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: A Home for the Holidays
programme.desc: The Robertsons help a long time employee whose Mom has fallen on hard times by buying her a brand new house for Christmas. Jep, Jase, Martin and Godwin race to finish a deck in time for the big reveal, only to get sidetracked by a hammering competition.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: The Cannonball Runs
programme.desc: Jase enters an outhouse race to help promote Willie's Duck Diner, but when the Robertsons show up for the race, they face an unexpected rival - Phillip McMillan. Meanwhile, Willie attempts to create the world's greatest bologna sandwich.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Mo Math, Mo Problems
programme.desc: Willie helps Sadie with her homework by enlisting the guys to create a real-life version of a math problem, but when Jase and Willie get sidetracked helping Sadie, they rekindle an old argument of whether Jase can outrun a truck.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Coop! There It Is!
programme.desc: When Jep asks Willie and the guys to help move his old chicken coop into his new house, which is under renovation, Jessica takes issue with its appearance, leading the guys to yet another project - renovating the chicken coop.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Friday Afternoon Lights
programme.desc: When Willie becomes an assistant coach on Lil' Will's struggling football team, his authority is tested when the kids challenge the adults to a scrimmage. Meanwhile, Kay brings Jep and Si to an aquarobics class at a local health club.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Master and Duck Commander
programme.desc: When Jase and the guys transform an old pontoon boat into a floating duck blind, they are faced with the impending question of whether or not it will actually float. Meanwhile, Kay's dog Bobo misbehaves.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Inlawful Entry
programme.desc: When Missy's parents visit, her father convinces Jase to take him on a quail hunt. But while their "bonding hunt" yields very few quail, it does bring them closer together as friends. Meanwhile, Willie helps John Luke start his own snow cone business.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Flock and Key

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Ball in the Family

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Drone Survivor

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Alan in Charge
programme.desc: Jase and the guys convince Willie to hire his older brother Alan to work at Duck Commander, but when Alan begins micromanaging everyone, they quickly change their mind. Meanwhile, the ladies take a self-defense class and use John Luke as a punching bag.

programme.title: Strange Evidence

programme.title: Strange Evidence

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Curse of the Monster Spider: A massive spider's web takes over a park, and experts investigate if this monster's trap could capture a human. A tree spurts out thick red blood.

programme.title: Strange Evidence

programme.title: Strange Evidence

programme.title: Strange Evidence

programme.title: Hitler & The Nazis
programme.desc: The fall of a hate-fueled and manipulative madman, and those who faithfully followed him from the invasion at Normandy, the death camps of Auschwitz-Birkenau, the military failure at Stalingrad, and the fall of Berlin.

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches
programme.desc: Experience first hand "the war to end all wars." WWI was the first war in history to affect most of the globe, with battles fought on land, at sea and, for the first time, in the sky.

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches
programme.desc: WW1 starts with the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria-Hungary in 1914, in Sarajevo. The assassination triggered a chain of events as Austria-Hungary declared war on Serbia, leading to a series of treaties being activated.

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches
programme.desc: The conflict sawa- the development of trench warfare, with major battles such as the Battle of the Marne, the First Battle of Ypres, and the Race to the Sea, leading to a stalemate that set the stage for the prolonged and devastating nature of the war.

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches
programme.desc: The conflict saw- the development of trench warfare, with major battles such as the Battle of the Marne, the First Battle of Ypres, and the Race to the Sea, leading to a stalemate that set the stage for the prolonged and devastating nature of the war.

programme.title: World War I in the Trenches

programme.title: Hell On Earth WWII
programme.desc: The war enters its third year with battlefields in Eastern Europe, North Africa and the Asia/Pacific. The Battle of Midway, a battle with irreversible consequences, is a victory for the USA.

programme.title: History's Greatest Aircraft

programme.title: Arthur: Muffy's Soccer Shocker/Brother, Can You Spare A Clarinet?
programme.desc: Muffy's proud that her dad is the new soccer coach--until he exhausts the team with demanding drills. The try-outs for the Young Person's Orchestra are coming to Lakewood Elementary and everyone agrees that Binky is a shoe-in.

programme.title: Arthur: Hic or Treat/Mr. Alwaysright
programme.desc: D.W. gets a case of the hiccups that just won't go away. Will she have them for the rest of her life and set the world record? It seems that Brain is always right about everything and it's really starting to get on Buster's nerves.

programme.title: Arthur: Sue Ellen's Lost Diary/Arthur's Knee
programme.desc: Poor Sue Ellen has lost the one thing she's never without--her diary. Then, Arthur's hurt his knee but won't tell Mom--because then she'd know he'd been playing down at the dump , where he was NOT allowed to go.

programme.title: Arthur: The Case of the Girl with the Long Face/The Substitute Arthur
programme.desc: Fern's been feeling low and no one can figure out why. Luckily, Buster Baxter is on the case! With Arthur out of town, Buster is on the hunt for a new best friend, but he only wants to play games HIS way. Will Buster learn to compromise?

programme.title: Arthur: Carl's Concerto/Too Much of a Good Thing
programme.desc: Carl has agreed to play the accordion for George's puppet show. But his routines are getting in the way of rehearsals. Buster enlists Binky's help to keep him from gorging on delicious Tuvaluna cookies. Will Buster's self-control crumble?

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Birthday/Francine Frensky, Superstar
programme.desc: Only four more days until Arthur's birthday party! But to Arthur's horror, it turns out Muffy's having her birthday party on the very same day. And, now that Francine finally has the lead in a school play, she's has become a stage tyrant.

programme.title: Arthur: Attack Of The Turbo Tibbles/D.W. Tricks The Tooth Fairy
programme.desc: If you think the Tibbles are bad now, imagine what they'd be like if they had super powers! Then, D.W. needs money, quick. When she tries to pull a fast one on the Tooth Fairy, will Arthur have to pay?

programme.title: Arthur: The Squirrels/Fern and Persimmony Glitchet
programme.desc: After watching the classic horror movie, "The Squirrels," Arthur and his friends are scared to step out into the playground, and everywhere else! Fern's story about perfect Felicity Bonchance is thought perfectly boring by her friends.

programme.title: Arthur: Two Minutes/Messy Dress Mess
programme.desc: Power shifts in the Tibble household when the twins find out Tommy is two minutes older than Timmy. Ladonna's thrilled when Muffy loans her a beautiful recital dress, but a run-in with a painted bench creates a problem.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: The Emperor's Contest - The Whole Truth
programme.desc: Everyone is excited about a fishing contest that is being held in the park; everyone except Ryan, who doesn't want to follow the contest rules. When Ryan is tempted not to follow the rules, Barney tells a story called "The Emperor's Contest."

programme.title: Barney and Friends: It's Hot! It's Cold!
programme.desc: Throughout the day, Barney and the children learn about "hot" and "cold," but the fun really begins when Barney turns the playground into a winter/summer wonderland!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After BJ loses everyone's tickets to the circus, he learns a valuable lesson about responsibility. Baby Bop discovers a lonely little flower struggling to grow in a shady spot.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When some of the children in the park invite Barney to join them for a race./When BJ starts to feel sick after eating too many sweets, he lies down in hopes of feeling better.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: You Can Count on Me
programme.desc: Barney helps his friends discover just how fun numbers can be by taking them on a number hunt in the park. Baby Bop delights in sharing her favorite number and the kids discover that numbers can be found everywhere.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Squares, Squares Everywhere
programme.desc: Nick's homework is to find as many squares as he can. It's great fun when Barney and the kids help!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When several sports balls suddenly disappear without a trace, it’s detective BJ to the rescue as he dons trenchcoat and fedora.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: My Family and Me
programme.desc: The children are invited to a family picnic in the park, but don't know who invited them. The invitations instruct them to bring a family treasure, so each child has something special with them. Barney helps lead a discussion about families.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Elementary, My Dear Turtle
programme.desc: The Turtles are in England, when they are taken back to the past, via an experimental atomic clock and must team up with Sherlock Holmes and Doctor Watson in order to get the clock back and return to the present.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Night of the Dark Turtle
programme.desc: When Donatello receives a jolt of electrical energy, he undergoes a radical personality transformation, turning into 'The Dark Turtle', a psychotic caped crimefighter on a mission of revenge against Shredder!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Starchild
programme.desc: The Turtles face a crisis of galactic proportions when they must play babysitter to an alien infant with incredible powers.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Legend of Koji
programme.desc: The Turtles take an incredible trip back in time when they follow Shredder back to 16th Century Japan, where they join forces with Splinter's ancestor, the youthful samurai koji, and help him found the Foot Clan.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Convicts from Dimension X
programme.desc: Donatello, tinkering with his InterDimensional Early Warning System device, accidently frees two vicious convicts from their maximum security cell on Dimension X, bringing them to earth.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): White Belt, Black Heart
programme.desc: Splinter's ancient sensei, Master Mogo-San, arrives from Japan, seeking to find his grandson, to hand him a precious family heirloom - a sacred white ninja belt.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Night of the Rogues
programme.desc: Determined to stop the turtles once and for all, Shredder enlists some of the most memorable villains from past episodes into an army of 'rogues'!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Attack of the Neutrinos
programme.desc: When Krang captures the Neutrino's, he subjects the fun-loving, hot-rodding teenagers from Dimension X to a brain treatment which turns them evil!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Escape from the Planet of the Turtleoids
programme.desc: The Turtles must once again help their Turtleoid friend Kurma defend the planet Shellri-La from alien invaders.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Revenge of the Fly
programme.desc: When Baxter Stockman, AKA the Fly, gets his pincers on some of Krang and Shredder's mutagen, he uses it to start turning everyone in the city into giant hideous mutant insects!

programme.desc: When Ash hears of a virtually unbeatable Trainer nearby, he's itching to take on the challenge. But both Brock’s Vulpix and Ash’s Cyndaquil take a beating from her Skarmory, even with their type advantage! How can Ash defeat it?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash’s Totodile develops a crush on a passing Azumarill, the star performer of a Pokémon circus. When Team Rocket arrives to steal the show, Azumarill’s heart isn't the only thing Totodile has to win!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash has his sights set on a rare Noctowl with unusual coloring, but this clever Pokémon repeatedly uses Hypnosis to get the better of him! Will Ash be able to catch this Noctowl napping?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: A surprise attack from a group of Ursaring has our heroes and Team Rocket running for cover. In the chaos, the groups are split up, and the unenthusiastic pairings are forced to work together. But for how long?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: After seeing a sign in the forest warning them about mischievous Ghost-type Pokémon, our heroes reach a town whose residents use Psychic types to repel them. But soon some spooky news arrives: a giant Gengar is on the loose!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: A sudden craze in Pokémon fortune-telling turns out to be a plot to steal Pokémon from local Trainers. But Jessie, James and Meowth aren't behind it this time! Who are these con artists?

programme.title: Pokemon: Johto League Champions
programme.desc: Arriving in Goldenrod City, Ash is ready for his next Gym battle, but the Gym appears to be closed. Their plans delayed, our heroes decide to explore the vibrant city.

programme.title: Pokemon: Johto League Champions

programme.title: Pokemon: Johto League Champions
programme.desc: With his new Gym badge in hand, Ash is asked to appear on a local radio station. But while he's acting out a radio drama with some 'professional actors,' the scripted danger becomes all too lifelike!

programme.title: Pokemon: Johto League Champions

programme.title: The Astral Factor

programme.desc: A blaxploitation film about a woman whose drug-dealing brother ratted on her undercover narcotics-agent boyfriend to the mob and she, in turn, poses as a call girl to infiltrate the mobsters who butchered him.

programme.title: The Magnificent Seven
programme.desc: Chanu, a young Indian from a local reservation has kidnapped Claire Moseley, the daughter of missionary Owen Mosely.

programme.desc: Imagine a prison so fierce that guards stay outside for fear of their lives. Inside, the prisoners are in control: murderers, violent offenders, drug bosses. The inmates rule and anarchy prevails. Only the strongest survive this lawless place.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Unabomber

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: That's got to be them!
